name: Advance America
description_short: One of the largest US short-term lenders, offering payday loans, installment loans, title loans, and lines of credit at 800+ stores across 22 states and online.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Greenville, South Carolina, Advance America is one of the largest short-term consumer lenders in the United States. The company operates under the parent brand "Purpose Financial" and is owned by Grupo Elektra, a large Mexican retail and financial conglomerate. Advance America holds state-issued consumer lending licenses in every state where it operates and has earned Great Place to Work® certification for two consecutive years — 2024 and 2025 — with an 84% employee approval rating. It carries a BBB A+ rating and has been BBB-accredited since September 2024.

The company's core product is the payday loan — a lump-sum advance due on the borrower's next payday (2–4 weeks), with fees running roughly $15 per $100 borrowed, though state-regulated rates range from $10 to $30 per $100, translating to APRs of approximately 350–700%. Beyond payday loans, Advance America offers installment loans with 3–24 month repayment terms, title loans secured by a vehicle title, and revolving lines of credit up to approximately $4,000 in select states. Cash advances and money order services complete the lineup. Approximately 800+ physical store locations across 22 states allow qualified borrowers to receive cash in about 30 minutes in-store, while an online platform covering 27 states delivers funds the next business day — or same-day for applications approved before noon.

A few policies stand out in a crowded industry. Advance America's "Worry-Free Advance" waives loan fees entirely if a borrower loses their job while the loan is outstanding — an unusually borrower-friendly provision for this segment. Repeat customers also see meaningfully higher approval rates (~51%) compared to first-time applicants (~39%). The company does not report loan activity to Equifax, Experian, or TransUnion, which means a missed payment won't further damage a credit score — though on-time payments won't help build credit history either. With nearly 30 years in operation, Advance America has the scale, name recognition, and operational consistency that smaller regional lenders lack.

The central limitation is cost. APRs of 350–700% make payday loans among the most expensive legal credit products available, and borrowers who can't repay on the due date risk compounding costs through rollovers. The BBB customer review score of 1.7 out of 5 — drawn from 154 reviews and 302 complaints over three years — signals a real gap between marketing and day-to-day customer experience. The "Worry-Free Advance" policy and workplace certifications are genuine positives, but Advance America remains a lender of last resort for most consumers. Anyone with access to a credit union PAL, employer advance, or a 0% intro credit card should exhaust those options first.
address: 1300 W Vandament Ave Unit 401, Yukon, OK 73099

pricing.guarantee_details: Worry-Free Advance: loan fees waived if borrower loses their job while the loan is outstanding. Early repayment may yield a partial fee refund in select states. Money order refunds available via form submission with a $15 non-refundable processing fee.

pros: 800+ physical store locations across 22 states with in-store cash disbursement in approximately 30 minutes
Online lending platform available in 27 states with next-business-day funding (same-day if approved before noon)
BBB A+ rating and accredited since September 2024
"Worry-Free Advance" waives all loan fees if borrower loses their job during the loan term
Founded in 1997 — nearly 30 years of consumer lending experience and operational scale
No credit bureau reporting — missed payments do not further damage an already low credit score
Great Place to Work® certified in both 2024 and 2025 with 84% employee approval
cons: Payday loan APRs of approximately 350–700% make these among the highest-cost legal credit products in the US
BBB customer review score of only 1.7/5 from 154 reviews, with 302 complaints filed in the past three years
No credit bureau reporting means on-time repayments do not help build or improve credit history
Product availability and rates vary significantly by state — not all loan types are offered everywhere
Ultimately owned by Grupo Elektra, a foreign conglomerate — no longer a US-independent company
best_for: Underbanked or credit-challenged consumers who need emergency cash within hours and have no lower-cost borrowing options
Borrowers facing a short-term cash shortfall between paychecks who can commit to repaying on their next payday
Consumers in one of 22 states with a nearby store who prefer face-to-face service and same-day cash
People who need a small loan and have already been declined by banks, credit unions, and credit cards
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers in a genuine short-term cash emergency who lack access to lower-cost credit and need funds within hours. The main caveat is price: at $15 per $100 borrowed, a two-week $300 loan costs $45 in fees, and an inability to repay on the due date can trigger costly rollovers. Borrowers should treat this as a true last resort and have a firm repayment plan in place before applying.
services: Payday Loans (2–4 week term, ~$15 per $100 borrowed, state-regulated fees)
Title Loans (vehicle-secured, rates vary by state)
Lines of Credit (revolving, up to ~$4,000 in select states)
In-store cash disbursement (~30 minutes after approval)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
